Transcrição Completa: 17 The Power Of Expert Positioning

 

 

 

[00:00] [music] There is so much power in positioning yourself as an expert comparatively to being the guy or girl who's just doing a bunch of random activities or actions or even worse the agency owner who has the belief that they are bigger than they are positioned to be.

 

 

 

[00:18] That's the worst. You have to position yourself to be an expert. You can't simply think you're an expert and then be an expert. It's about positioning.

 

 

 

[00:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Expert Positioning vs. Random Activities"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

 

 

VISUAL: Comparação visual entre posicionamento de especialista versus ações aleatórias, conceito de percepção vs. realidade no mercado

 

 

 

[00:30] You see, I can't be a specialist if I'm not positioned as a specialist. I can't be a swimmer like Michael Phelps in the Olympics if I'm not positioned as that role for what is incorporated with it.

 

 

 

[00:42] And more importantly, the positioning that makes it known that you are that which you identify with.

 

 

 

[00:48] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Becoming an Expert = Positioning Strategy"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

 

 

VISUAL: Framework de especialização: conhecimento + posicionamento + reconhecimento público, estrutura hierárquica de credibilidade

 

 

 

[00:50] So, becoming an expert, becoming somebody who's a specialist, becoming somebody who can get paid more for knowing more, all comes down to how you position yourself.

 

 

 

[01:00] There's a few key things here that I'm going to talk to you about, then I'm going to give you some actions that you could take at the end of this to help with your overall positioning.

 

 

 

[01:08] Positioning in a nutshell, most people think when they want to be an expert that they need to all of a sudden just produce all this content and all this knowledge in one particular area.

 

 

 

[01:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Content Production vs. Strategic Positioning"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

 

 

VISUAL: Comparação entre produção massiva de conteúdo e posicionamento estratégico, elementos de percepção pública

 

 

 

[01:20] And that can be true. That can be an effective strategy and it can obviously make it known to the people that you're distributing that to that you know those things.

 

 

 

[01:28] But then people will look at you in all kinds of different lights. They'll look how many followers you have. They'll look how much social interaction you have per post.

 

 

 

[01:36] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Social Proof Credibility Factors"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Seguidores, engajamento por post, resultados de busca

 

 

 

VISUAL: Lista de critérios de avaliação: número de seguidores, interações sociais, qualidade de fotos, presença no Google, resultados de busca

 

 

 

[01:38] Have you ever tried to pitch a client and then they say, "Well, you know, you want to help me with my social media, but I just checked your profile and it looks like you took a selfie on a $50 Android phone and like you have two photos.

 

 

 

[01:51] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Client Objection: Poor Personal Branding"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: $50 Android phone, 2 fotos

 

 

 

VISUAL: Exemplo visual de perfil amador vs. profissional, contraste entre baixa qualidade e alta qualidade de apresentação

 

 

 

[01:53] How can I trust you that you know about social media when you don't you're not positioned to talk to me like you know what you're talking about?

 

 

 

[02:01] Let's say you have no Google search results. Everybody who if I look up your name, it's all the other people that are named your name. You know, you have nothing in top search results. You're not even on page one yet. That's positioning.

 

 

 

[02:14] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Google Search Presence = Positioning Power"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Página 1 do Google

 

 

 

VISUAL: Representação de resultados de busca do Google, ausência vs. presença na primeira página, domínio de nome próprio

 

 

 

[02:16] You know you on YouTube. If I go look up your name, if I look up your agency, can I find anything? Is it going to be good? Is it going to be bad? That's positioning. Okay.

 

 

 

[02:25] How you are in real life? how much time availability you have can actually get you paid more as well.

 

 

 

[02:31] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Scarcity = Value: Time Availability Strategy"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

 

 

VISUAL: Conceito de escassez de tempo gerando valor premium, relação inversamente proporcional entre disponibilidade e valor percebido

 

 

 

[02:33] Positioning, an example of that in a literal sense is that having less time available makes the time that you make available worth more.

 

 

 

[02:42] So therefore, positioning yourself whether it's true or not that you don't have a lot of time to give out and that the time that you are giving out is worth a lot because you help people get results.

 

 

 

[02:53] That is an example of how to position yourself for being an expert because experts wouldn't have a lot of time because a true expert would have a lot of people taking their time because they know a lot in a particular area that can help drive people more leads, more money or do whatever the experts position to help with.

 

 

 

[03:11] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Expert Positioning Formula"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

 

 

VISUAL: Fórmula: Alta demanda + Tempo limitado = Valor premium, ciclo de credibilidade do especialista

 

 

 

[03:13] Positioning is what it comes down to. Okay? And the quality that you are producing content, the quality and how you are doing these things all starts to matter.

 

 

 

[03:23] There's a lot of simple things you could do at the bottom to start positioning yourself better.

 

 

 

[03:28] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Platform Certifications Strategy"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

 

 

VISUAL: Lista de certificações disponíveis: Google Analytics, Google Adwords, Facebook Blueprint, badges de certificação

 

 

 

[03:30] If you want to claim as an agency owner that you know how to do things like set up Google Analytics, you know how to master Google Adwords and you you can bring people more leads and make them more money with that.

 

 

 

[03:42] If you want to say that you can do Facebook ads for people, but you don't even have a client yet, the best thing for you to do is to go get certified from those platforms.

 

 

 

[03:51] Why? Because they give you a badge that says this person took this course and is certified to be a Facebook blueprint certification, a Google Adwords certification, a Google Analytics certification.

 

 

 

[04:03] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "DigitalMarketer.com Certification System"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Múltiplas certificações especializadas

 

 

 

VISUAL: Plataforma DigitalMarketer.com, lista de especializações: email marketing, advertising, paid traffic, community management, social media management, badges individuais

 

 

 

[04:05] There's websites like digitalmarketer.com for each specific course that they have like email marketing, advertising, paid traffic, community management, social media management.

 

 

 

[04:17] Every time you take one of those individual mastery courses they have, they give you an individual badge that says you are a customer acquisition specialist, you are email marketing specialist, all of a sudden these badges that you're accumulating, especially when you're first getting started, now you're actually positioned to be an expert.

 

 

 

[04:34] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Badge Display Strategy: Email Footer"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

 

 

VISUAL: Exemplo de assinatura de email com badges de certificação, posicionamento visual de credenciais, impacto imediato em cada comunicação

 

 

 

[04:36] How do you make it known though that you've got that positioning? Simple. in your email footer. You can add those badges in and boom, everybody who you email now can recognize how you're positioned, what you know, and you've made it known that you've got those certifications and that you know those areas and that you're an expert in those areas compared to all the other people who likely haven't even logged into that same program.

 

 

 

[04:59] If their staff hasn't taken that, but you have, you'll have more value and you'll be more positioned as an expert in that one area, which will make them hear you out more in their head. Right?

 

 

 

[05:09] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Press and Media Credibility"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

 

 

VISUAL: Logos de publicações: Forbes, Inc., Entrepreneur.com, Huffington Post, estratégia de credibilidade por associação

 

 

 

[05:11] People do this all the time with press. You get in Forbes, Inc. Entrepreneur.com, right? Huffington Post. You say, "Hey, you know, I've been in for I've been in this. I've been in that. I've been in that."

 

 

 

[05:22] And then all of a sudden to people who don't know you, you have a higher level of credibility.

 

 

 

[05:27] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Press Credibility Warning"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

 

 

VISUAL: Alerta sobre credibilidade paga, diferenciação entre press releases pagos e conquistas reais

 

 

 

[05:29] Nowadays, I want to be clear, there's so many people that to that do that. And there's so many people that you can pay off to do that that it's not as credible as it once was.

 

 

 

[05:38] But to people who don't know that you can pay off people to do those things, that would still hold high credibility.

 

 

 

[05:44] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Inc. 500 vs. Inc. 5000 Distinction"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Inc. 500, Inc. 5000

 

 

 

VISUAL: Diferenciação entre listas de prestígio, hierarquia de credibilidade empresarial

 

 

 

[05:46] As an example, maybe you're a part of the Inc. 500 list or the Inc. 5,000 list. That'd be a key difference there for how you'd be positioned and how your business could be recognized instantly.

 

 

 

[05:56] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Local Credibility Building: Chamber of Commerce"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Algumas centenas de dólares de investimento

 

 

 

VISUAL: Estrutura de câmaras de comércio: nível local, estadual, provincial, nacional, badges de acreditação

 

 

 

[05:58] There's things that can get you credibility. Joining your local chamber of commerce. I know there's a lot of people in different countries from all over the planet here.

 

 

 

[06:06] But to be very clear, some countries will allow you like the US, Canada. I know a lot of countries in Europe have this. On a on a country level, a state level, on a province level, you can join in on a city level, on a local level.

 

 

 

[06:19] I mean, you can join into a list, pay a couple hundred bucks, and position yourself as somebody who's an accredited business.

 

 

 

[06:26] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "BBB Accreditation Strategy"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Algumas centenas de dólares

 

 

 

VISUAL: Logo Better Business Bureau, processo de auditoria e certificação, badge BBB accredited

 

 

 

[06:28] BBB, better business bureau. You can pay them for them to audit your business to show that you're certified as somebody who's BBB accredited.

 

 

 

[06:37] This is all things that are very cheap, effective ways to position yourself as in order to gain more expert credibility. Right?

 

 

 

[06:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Social Engagement Strategy"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: 1 like, 40 views vs. centenas de engajamentos, 10+ comentários

 

 

 

VISUAL: Comparação de métricas de engajamento: baixo engajamento vs. engajamento pago estratégico, impacto na percepção

 

 

 

[06:47] If I'm posting content and I'm talking about how a dentist or a chiropractor can make more money using a advertising or marketing strategy than I'm talking about my video, but my video gets one like and 40 views that you're not going to look at it the way that they would if I would pay a little bit of money to distribute that video, build a small social following and get even a couple hundred engagements on that.

 

 

 

[07:10] you know, the reactions. Even just 10 comments or so can make a big difference to somebody's interpretation of the level that you're currently operating at and what you know and what you don't know.

 

 

 

[07:20] These are all very cost-effective ways to position yourself.

 

 

 

[07:24] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Inc.com/verify Strategy"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: $30 investimento

 

 

 

VISUAL: Plataforma Inc.com/verify, badge de certificação "Incified", processo de submissão de negócio

 

 

 

[07:26] Um, I got to give my got to give credit to a guy Ulyses Osunu. He's a PR guy. He was speaking at the Internet Earner Summit in 2018 and he dropped this tip on the crowd. I thought it was incredible.

 

 

 

[07:38] He said a great way to position yourself. Inc.com/verify. So, inc.com/verify, you can for 30 bucks, you can submit your business and become certified from inc.com, which does the Inc. 5000 and Inc. 500 list and you get a badge.

 

 

 

[07:55] You just automatically get a badge that says you're incified. Same thing because that company's recognized, you position yourself to be credited by that company, now you get a little badge that shows it.

 

 

 

[08:05] Although you paid 30 bucks for it, that in somebody's mind builds trust.

 

 

 

[08:10] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Cold Email Qualification Process"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

 

 

VISUAL: Processo de qualificação de cold emails, elementos que prospects avaliam: domínio de email, foto de contato, presença profissional

 

 

 

[08:12] So, if you're sending out cold emails to people, they're going to look you up. If you're sending out cold emails, the only way that they have an ability to qualify you is based on how you position yourself.

 

 

 

[08:23] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Gmail vs. Custom Domain"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

 

 

VISUAL: Comparação visual: email@gmail.com vs. email@suaempresa.com, percepção de profissionalismo vs. amadorismo

 

 

 

[08:25] Let's say you're to send in from a regular Gmail instead of an at whatever your business name is.com. That's an example of positioning. One shows amateur-ish and one shows that you at least went as far as making yourself more professional.

 

 

 

[08:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Contact Photo Strategy"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

 

 

VISUAL: Presença vs. ausência de foto de contato profissional, impacto na credibilidade

 

 

 

[08:40] Maybe you don't have a contact photo for your email. That'd be another example of positioning yourself.

 

 

 

[08:45] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Professional Photography Investment"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: $25-$50 fotógrafo local, algumas centenas de dólares fotógrafo profissional

 

 

 

VISUAL: Comparação: selfie em Android $50 vs. headshots profissionais, action shots, sessão de fotos em locações

 

 

 

[08:47] Maybe the photos that you're taking, as I described, look like they're on a $50 Android. Go get some head shot taken. Go get some professional action shots taken. Pay a photographer couple hundred bucks.

 

 

 

[08:59] I almost guarantee that you went to high school with about 10 different people who now call themselves photographers that you could probably pay 25 to 50 bucks to that could take some killer shots of you out in a field somewhere or out in your local downtown that could make you look like an all-star comparatively to a few random selfies that you've got taken throughout the years. Right?

 

 

 

[09:21] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Final Positioning Principle"

 

 

 

NÚMEROS: Não especificados

 

 

 

VISUAL: Mensagem central: "Position Yourself as Expert, Don't Just Claim It", ciclo de credibilidade e reconhecimento

 

 

 

[09:23] Position yourself to be an expert. Don't just say you're an expert. Position yourself to be an expert. That's the name of the game.

 

 

 

[09:32] If you have any questions on this, comment below.

 

 

 

 

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

 

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

$30 - Investimento Inc.com/verify

$25-$50 - Custo fotógrafo local

$50 - Referência Android de baixa qualidade

Algumas centenas de dólares - Investimento em fotografia profissional

Algumas centenas de dólares - Custo de certificações e acreditações

1 like e 40 views - Exemplo de baixo engajamento

10+ comentários - Engajamento mínimo para credibilidade

Centenas de engajamentos - Meta de engajamento pago

Página 1 Google - Objetivo de presença online

 

 

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Certificações de Plataforma: Google Analytics, Google Adwords, Facebook Blueprint

Certificações DigitalMarketer.com: Email marketing, advertising, paid traffic, community management, social media management

Estratégia de Press Release: Forbes, Inc., Entrepreneur.com, Huffington Post

Acreditações Locais: Chamber of Commerce (municipal, estadual, provincial, nacional)

BBB Accreditation: Better Business Bureau

Inc.com/verify: Certificação por $30

Email Footer Strategy: Display de badges de certificação

Engajamento Social Pago: Distribuição de conteúdo para aumentar métricas

Fotografia Profissional: Headshots e action shots

Domínio Próprio: Email corporativo vs. Gmail

Presença no Google: SEO para primeira página

Presença no YouTube: Canal profissional

 

 

 

ESTRUTURA:

Posicionamento como Especialista (não apenas ser especialista)

Escassez de Tempo = Valor Premium

Qualificação Visual: Fotos, perfis sociais, presença online

Credibilidade por Associação: Certificações, press, acreditações

Prova Social: Engajamento, seguidores, comentários

Profissionalismo Digital: Domínio próprio, foto de contato, assinatura de email

 

 

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Investimentos de Baixo Custo:

Inc.com/verify: $30

Fotógrafo local: $25-$50

Fotógrafo profissional: Algumas centenas

Certificações: Algumas centenas

Chamber of Commerce: Algumas centenas

BBB: Algumas centenas

ROI de Posicionamento:

Tempo escasso = Valor premium

Certificações = Diferenciação competitiva

Profissionalismo visual = Maior taxa de conversão em cold emails

 

 

 

DURAÇÃO: 09:32 FORMATO: Vídeo educacional sobre posicionamento de especialista para donos de agência
